assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The intragroup dividend has led to recognition of an intragroup receivable/payable before consolidation eliminations.

approaches.label: Hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 provides the top-level accounting model for designating hedged items in consolidated financial statements, including limited cases involving intragroup foreign currency exposures.

approaches.label: Consolidation accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 10 requires full elimination of intragroup assets, liabilities, income, expenses and cash flows in consolidated financial statements, which is directly relevant to an intragroup dividend receivable.

approaches.label: Separate financial statements accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 27 sets out the accounting model for recognising dividends from subsidiaries in separate financial statements, which is relevant because the question refers to a receivable being recognised before considering consolidation.
